intent: Define the SecurityRecipes agentic control plane blueprint: a board-, buyer-, and engineering-ready architecture that connects open knowledge, secure context provenance, MCP authorization, connector trust, non-human identity, memory boundaries, egress policy, run receipts, red-team replay, and measurement probes into one defensible product narrative.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, Product Security, AppSec, GRC, model risk, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Secure context and control plane for agentic AI
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes turns agent context into an inspectable control plane: every workflow, source, connector, identity, tool decision, memory class, egress path, and run receipt can be queried as structured MCP evidence before agents act.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI adoption is shifting from isolated assistants to autonomous systems that call tools, persist memory, use MCP connectors, and operate through delegated authority. The durable product wedge is a vendor-neutral control layer that makes context, authorization, and evidence easy for security teams and agent hosts to consume.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative
standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns the blueprint to industry-led agent standards, community-led protocols, secure agent identity, agent authentication, and security evaluations.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI 800-2 Practices for Automated Benchmark Evaluations
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports reproducible automated benchmark and agent-system evaluations through measurement probes, red-team replay, and evidence reconstruction.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps secure context and generated evidence to GAI provenance, third-party components, monitoring, measurement, and risk-response expectations.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Covers agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, agentic supply-chain risk, unexpected code execution, memory and context poisoning, insecure inter-agent communication, cascading failures, human trust exploitation, and rogue-agent containment.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Anchors MCP authorization in OAuth 2.1, protected resource metadata, client metadata documents, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE S256, HTTPS, and forbidden token passthrough.

standards_alignment.name: CSA Securing the Agentic Control Plane
standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns the product thesis to identity, authorization, orchestration, runtime behavior, risk intelligence, certification, and trust assurance layers for autonomous AI agent ecosystems.

standards_alignment.name: CSA The AI Agent Governance Gap: What CISOs Need Now
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports the near-term need for internal agent governance while formal agent-specific standards and enforceable controls continue to mature.

standards_alignment.name: CSA AI Controls Matrix
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps the architecture to vendor-neutral AI control objectives, AI-CAIQ style assessment evidence, role accountability, and trustworthy AI operations.

board_level_success_criteria: Every agentic workflow has declared ownership, scope, gates, KPIs, and kill signals.
Every context source has owner, trust tier, source hash, poisoning controls, citation requirements, and retrieval policy.
Every MCP namespace has connector trust, authorization conformance, gateway policy, and scoped access mode.
Every agent run can be reconstructed through identity, context, tool, egress, approval, verifier, closure, and revocation evidence.
Every scale decision is backed by readiness, capability risk, red-team replay, measurement probes, and current threat-radar alignment.

layers.title: Workflow Scope and Default-Deny Control
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer see exactly which workflows agents may run, which gates apply, and why undeclared actions fail closed?
layers.premium_path: Hosted workflow promotion gates, customer evidence ingestion, and policy drift alerts.
layers.exit_value: Shows the project is an enforceable control plane, not a static prompt library.

layers.title: Secure Context Provenance
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer prove which context an agent received, who owns it, how fresh it is, which attestation hash applies, and how retrieved text is demoted from instruction to evidence?
layers.premium_path: Signed context releases, tenant context registries, source-hash recertification, context attestation APIs, and retrieval approval workflows.
layers.exit_value: Creates a durable data moat around trusted agent context.

layers.title: MCP Authorization and Connector Trust
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer approve remote MCP servers without token passthrough, audience confusion, scope creep, shadow tools, or unreviewed connector drift?
layers.premium_path: Remote connector inventory, OAuth metadata checks, scope-drift detection, and connector trust-center export.
layers.exit_value: Directly maps to the MCP ecosystem problem large AI labs and platforms need solved.

layers.title: Agent Identity and Delegation
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer identify which non-human identity acted, which human owner delegated authority, which scopes were denied, and how unsafe sessions are revoked?
layers.premium_path: OIDC, SPIFFE/SPIRE, source-host, and MCP gateway identity adapters for per-run authority.
layers.exit_value: Turns non-human identity for agents into a governed security category.

layers.title: Memory, Skill, and Runtime Boundaries
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer prevent persistent memory, vector recall, skills, hooks, and rules files from quietly inheriting agent authority?
layers.premium_path: Hosted skill registry scanning, memory policy enforcement, vector-memory approvals, and drift replay tests.
layers.exit_value: Covers the next agent supply-chain surface before most enterprises have tooling.

layers.title: Context Egress and Data Boundaries
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer prove context does not leave tenant, model-provider, telemetry, MCP, or public-corpus boundaries without data-class and destination policy?
layers.premium_path: DLP, DPA, zero-data-retention, telemetry, and residency policy exports for enterprise AI platforms.
layers.exit_value: Makes security and privacy teams comfortable enough to approve agentic context at scale.

layers.title: Evidence Receipts and Agentic System BOM
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer reconstruct what an agent saw, which tools it used, which policies allowed it, who approved it, and which component changed?
layers.premium_path: Customer trust-center exports, trace retention, audit-ready receipts, and BOM drift notifications.
layers.exit_value: Converts agent operations into buyer- and auditor-readable proof.

layers.title: Measurement, Red-Team Replay, and Threat Alignment
layers.proof_question: Can the buyer show that current threat signals become probes, drills, readiness decisions, and roadmap actions instead of stale docs?
layers.premium_path: Hosted replay runner, model and connector drift probes, executive threat posture reports, and risk trend dashboards.
layers.exit_value: Shows a path from open knowledge to recurring enterprise control-plane revenue.

buyer_due_diligence_questions.question: Which context sources are allowed into an agent run, and how is source drift detected?
How does the product prevent token passthrough, wrong-audience tokens, scope creep, and unreviewed remote MCP tools?
Who owns an agent identity, what can it do, and how is one unsafe run revoked?
Can the team reconstruct the exact policy, context, tool, approval, verifier, and egress path after an incident?
Which workflows are ready to scale, which stay in a guarded pilot, and which are blocked?

production_mcp_server: Connector trust and authorization conformance APIs
Run receipt storage and trust-center export
enterprise_expansion: Hosted red-team replay and measurement probes
Procurement, GRC, and AI platform reporting
strategic_acquirer_fit: Frontier model labs need safe tool and context layers around agents.
AI coding platforms need buyer-grade evidence before enterprise expansion.
Cloud and security platforms need MCP authorization, connector trust, and non-human identity controls.
The open corpus creates adoption while hosted evidence, policy, and MCP operations create enterprise value.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes is the secure context and control plane for agentic AI, with machine-readable evidence for context, MCP authorization, identity, memory, egress, receipts, measurement, and readiness.
default_questions_answered: Which standards and current agentic security guidance does it track?
Which MCP tools expose the evidence?
Which generated packs prove control coverage?
What is the credible path from open project to enterprise revenue?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the generated blueprint to AI platform architecture review, enterprise MCP server intake, procurement security review, quarterly agentic threat model, and acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open secure-context evidence, then sell hosted context signing, MCP authorization conformance, connector trust monitoring, trace ingestion, red-team replay, and trust-center exports.
